---
title: "UIAO Authorization Boundary"
version: "1.0"
classification: "CUI"
diagram-owner: gemini
---

# UIAO Authorization Boundary

**Version 1.0 | Classification: CUI | FedRAMP Moderate**

---

## 1. Boundary Overview

### 1.1 System Identification

| Field | Value |
|:---|:---|
| System Name | Unified Identity-Addressing-Overlay (UIAO) |
| Acronym | UIAO |
| Authorization Level | FedRAMP Moderate (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5) |
| Boundary Owner | UIAO Program Office |
| Classification | Public |

### 1.2 Boundary Definition

The UIAO authorization boundary encompasses all hardware, software, infrastructure, data,
personnel, procedures, and facilities required to operate the system that are under the direct
management and security control of the system owner.

**Inside the boundary:**
- All control plane services managed by the agency (Identity, Addressing, Overlay, Telemetry)
- Identity provider and ICAM infrastructure
- Network fabric — SD-WAN nodes under agency management
- IPAM and DNS platform instances
- SIEM and telemetry aggregation infrastructure
- Policy engine and orchestration management plane

**Outside the boundary (leveraged services):**
- FedRAMP-authorized cloud service providers (CSPs) with their own ATO packages
- Third-party SaaS tools with separate authorization boundaries
- Agency shared physical infrastructure

---

## 2. Authorization Boundary Diagram

The diagram below provides the authoritative depiction of the UIAO authorization boundary
for inclusion in the FedRAMP System Security Plan (SSP).

```diagram
UIAO Authorization Boundary diagram for a FedRAMP Moderate federal system security plan.
Use a clean rectangular boundary box labeled "UIAO Authorization Boundary (FedRAMP Moderate)" in dark blue.
OUTSIDE the boundary (top section, light gray background):
  - "End Users (Remote / Branch)" on the left
  - "Internet / WAN" in the center
  - "FedRAMP-Authorized CSPs (Azure GovCloud / AWS GovCloud)" on the right
INSIDE the boundary (main box, white background), show five swim-lane rows:
  Row 1 — Perimeter: "Next-Gen Firewall" | "UIAO API Gateway (TLS Termination)"
  Row 2 — Identity Plane: "Microsoft Entra ID (IdP / SSO)" | "ICAM Platform" | "CyberArk PAM"
  Row 3 — Network/Overlay Plane: "Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN" | "ZTNA Micro-Segmentation"
  Row 4 — Addressing Plane: "InfoBlox IPAM" | "DNS / DHCP"
  Row 5 — Telemetry Plane: "Microsoft Sentinel (SIEM)" | "CDM/CLAW"
Draw arrows from outside users through the perimeter into the Identity Plane, then to the other planes.
Add dashed arrow from CSPs labeled "Inherited Controls" pointing into the boundary.
Label each boundary-crossing arrow with the protocol (HTTPS/TLS 1.3, SAML, IPFIX, etc.).
White background, professional federal government SSP quality, clean sans-serif typography.
Include "FedRAMP Moderate" badge in the top-right corner.
```

---

## 3. External Connections

### 3.1 External Connection Inventory

All connections crossing the UIAO authorization boundary are documented below and reviewed
quarterly. All data in transit is encrypted with TLS 1.3 or IPSec.

| Connection | Direction | Protocol | Encryption | Risk |
|:---|:---|:---|:---|:---|
| Agency Staff → UIAO Gateway | Inbound | HTTPS 443 | TLS 1.3 | Medium |
| UIAO → Entra ID (Microsoft 365) | Outbound | HTTPS 443 | TLS 1.3 | Low |
| UIAO → InfoBlox BloxOne | Outbound | HTTPS / DNS | TLS 1.3 / DNSSEC | Low |
| UIAO → Cisco vManage | Bidirectional | HTTPS / NETCONF | TLS 1.3 | Low |
| UIAO → Microsoft Sentinel | Outbound | HTTPS 443 | TLS 1.3 | Low |
| UIAO → CDM/CLAW | Outbound | HTTPS 443 | TLS 1.3 | Low |
| UIAO → Azure GovCloud | Bidirectional | HTTPS / ExpressRoute | TLS 1.3 / IPSec | Medium |

### 3.2 Inherited Controls

The following FedRAMP-authorized providers contribute inherited controls to the UIAO boundary:

| Provider | Product | FedRAMP Level | Package |
|:---|:---|:---|:---|
| Microsoft | Azure Government (Entra ID + Sentinel) | High Authorized | Azure Government |
| Cisco | Catalyst SD-WAN | Moderate Authorized | Cisco SD-WAN FedRAMP |
| InfoBlox | BloxOne DDI | Moderate Authorized | InfoBlox BloxOne FedRAMP |
| CyberArk | Privileged Access Manager | Moderate Authorized | CyberArk PAM FedRAMP |

---

## 4. Boundary Crossing Data Flows

### 4.1 Data Flow Diagram

The diagram below traces all data flows that cross the UIAO authorization boundary, showing
data type, classification, direction, and encryption method for each flow.

```diagram
UIAO Authorization Boundary data flow diagram for a federal ATO package.
Show a vertical split layout with a bold dashed boundary line in the center.
LEFT side (Outside Boundary, light gray):
  - "Agency Users" (person icons) at top-left
  - "Branch Offices" (building icons) at bottom-left
  - "Azure GovCloud" (cloud icon) at right-center
RIGHT side (Inside Boundary, white):
  - "UIAO Gateway" at the top receiving traffic
  - Below it: "Identity Plane" (Entra ID), "Network Plane" (SD-WAN), "Data Layer", "Telemetry (Sentinel)"
Label each boundary-crossing arrow with:
  - Arrow direction
  - Data type (e.g., "SAML Assertion", "IPFIX Flow Records", "OSCAL Evidence")
  - Classification (CUI)
  - Encryption method (TLS 1.3)
Professional federal government SSP aesthetic, white background, clean typography.
```

### 4.2 Data Classification at the Boundary

| Data Type | Classification | Direction | Encryption | Purpose |
|:---|:---|:---|:---|:---|
| Authentication Tokens (JWT/SAML) | CUI | Inbound / Outbound | TLS 1.3 | Identity federation |
| Network Flow Records (IPFIX) | CUI | Outbound | TLS 1.3 | SIEM telemetry |
| DNS Query Logs | CUI | Outbound | TLS 1.3 | Threat correlation |
| Device Compliance Status | CUI | Inbound | TLS 1.3 | Posture validation |
| OSCAL Evidence Artifacts | CUI | Outbound | TLS 1.3 | Continuous authorization |
| SD-WAN Configuration | CUI | Outbound | TLS 1.3 | vManage control plane |

---

## 5. Ports, Protocols, and Services

### 5.1 Allowed Inbound

| Port | Protocol | Service | Source | Justification |
|:---|:---|:---|:---|:---|
| 443 | TCP/HTTPS | UIAO Gateway | Agency users | Application access (TLS 1.3) |
| 443 | TCP/HTTPS | Entra ID federation | Microsoft cloud | SSO / SAML delivery |
| 53 | UDP/DNS | InfoBlox DNS | Internal clients | Name resolution (DNSSEC) |

### 5.2 Allowed Outbound

| Port | Protocol | Service | Destination | Justification |
|:---|:---|:---|:---|:---|
| 443 | TCP/HTTPS | Microsoft Sentinel | Azure GovCloud | Log ingestion |
| 443 | TCP/HTTPS | InfoBlox BloxOne | InfoBlox Cloud | IPAM synchronization |
| 443 | TCP/HTTPS | Cisco vManage | Cisco Cloud | SD-WAN control plane |
| 443 | TCP/HTTPS | CDM/CLAW | DHS CDM SaaS | Asset visibility |
| 500/4500 | UDP/IKEv2 | SD-WAN IPSec | Branch / WAN | Encrypted WAN tunnels |

### 5.3 Blocked Traffic

All traffic not explicitly permitted by the above allowlists is denied at the perimeter firewall
and UIAO Gateway. All denied traffic events are logged to Microsoft Sentinel for analysis and
alerting.

---

## 6. Related Documents

- `src/templates/system-architecture.md` — Full four-plane system architecture
- `src/templates/control-plane-architecture.md` — Detailed control plane architecture
- `docs/fedramp_ssp_narrative_full_v1.0.md` — Complete SSP narrative
- `docs/system_inventory_and_components_v1.0.md` — Component inventory
- `exports/oscal/` — Machine-readable OSCAL boundary artifacts

---

*Source: UIAO-Core Architecture — Authorization Boundary v1.0*
